125689.explanation: Venray Lokaal believes that administrators should be elected based on quality. We prefer a good mix, but imposing fixed proportions does not fit with how we view good governance.

125690.explanation: The voice of residents is important. That is why they choose their representatives in elections. Referendums can raise expectations that are not always met. We opt for earlier involvement and better explanation.

125693.explanation: There must be room for agricultural entrepreneurship, but not everything fits everywhere within existing rules. Venray Lokaal wants to assess what is appropriate per location.

125694.explanation: If residents want to generate sustainable energy together, that deserves support. Venray Lokaal believes in local initiatives and feasible steps that fit Venray.

125696.explanation: If reception functions well and there is little nuisance, closure is not automatic. Venray Lokaal chooses a careful consideration, with attention to the environment and legal obligations.

125697.explanation: Employers are responsible for good housing, but not everything needs to be on their own site. Separation of living and working can actually contribute to the livability of the area, and to employees and fair distribution.

125701.explanation: A central fireworks display can ensure safety and collective celebration, especially due to the national ban. That fits with Venray, provided it is well organized.

125705.explanation: Under conditions, building on agricultural land can contribute to a future for farmers and villages. We consider per situation what fits within existing regulations and with the landscape and surroundings.

125706.explanation: Sport is important for health and social interaction and works preventively. Money should not be a reason to not be able to participate. Venray Lokaal believes everyone should have the chance to play sports. Also because this ultimately saves costs on the back end.

125713.explanation: Safety requires visible enforcement. Venray Lokaal wants sufficient public order officers to act in cases of nuisance and insecurity so residents feel comfortable on the street and in their neighborhood.

125714.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety in specific locations. Venray Lokaal deploys this targeted, with clear rules and attention to privacy.

125715.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be helped quickly and safely. Venray Lokaal considers good shelter essential but assesses the best form per situation. This can be a dedicated location or regional cooperation.

125719.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended as permanent residences. Venray Lokaal sees at most temporary space to relieve housing pressure for Venray residents, as long as this does not attract additional labor migration and stops once sufficient homes are built.

125721.explanation: Participating is important. Those who can should contribute to society. Venray Lokaal believes this should happen meaningfully, appropriately, and with regard to personal circumstances.

125725.explanation: Electric driving is growing. Venray Lokaal wants sufficient charging points, considering accessibility, space, and a good distribution across neighborhoods and villages.

125727.explanation: Care must align with who someone is. Venray Lokaal believes people should, where possible, be able to choose care that fits their faith, culture, or life philosophy.

125731.explanation: The tourist tax was recently adjusted and also applies to labor migrants. This has brought it back to an appropriate level. Venray Lokaal wants to keep Venray attractive to visitors and sees no reason for a new increase now.

125732.explanation: Venray entrepreneurs are looking for space. Employment needs space. Venray Lokaal wants to develop space for companies if there is demonstrable demand and if it fits the surroundings and infrastructure. Development is already happening based on demand.

125733.explanation: More shopping Sundays give entrepreneurs and residents flexibility and create a lively center. Venray Lokaal wants to allow this without making it mandatory.

125734.explanation: Venray Lokaal seeks a careful balance between legal obligations and housing opportunities for local residents. No simple yes/no, but tailor-made solutions, also because otherwise the shelter problem will only grow.

125736.explanation: Venray Lokaal does not prohibit this in advance. We first choose solar on roofs and facades but consider per location whether agricultural land may be suitable, also to provide space for retiring farmers.

125737.explanation: Venray Lokaal believes in initiatives from below. Through Schoon door de Poort, beautiful projects have already been realized. Therefore, we don't renew everything by default but look where there is need and support.

125738.explanation: Swimming safety is important. Venray Lokaal believes children should have the chance to learn to swim well, regardless of their home situation.

125739.explanation: The Venray dialect is part of our identity. As a local party, we want to provide space for the regional language in schools in a fitting and accessible way.

125743.explanation: Village centers are important for social interaction. With Schoon door de Poort, villages have taken responsibility for their community centers. Venray Lokaal continues to support where necessary but does not automatically intervene everywhere.

125745.explanation: More parking spaces are not always the solution. For housing construction, Venray Lokaal wants more modern parking standards that allow space for shared use, shared mobility, and bicycles. This keeps neighborhoods livable and allows for faster construction.

125746.explanation: A completely car-free center does not fit with Venray. Venray Lokaal wants a center that remains accessible to everyone and to maintain it as it is now.

125747.explanation: There must always be sheltered workplaces in Venray. Venray Lokaal wants people with a work disability to always have suitable work. The form is less important than the result: no one should fall through the cracks.

125749.explanation: The current system causes nuisance. Venray Lokaal wants to first evaluate what works and, because collection costs are directly charged to residents, not switch to waste bins at any cost.

125750.explanation: More military use has a major impact on the environment. Venray Lokaal does not want to accept this easily and remains critical of the consequences for livability.

125689.explanation: We find it important that women also make up part of the new council, but we do not believe that a number or quota should be fixed.

125693.explanation: Pushing some things too far goes too far; we do find it good to support farmers with possible side activities. Where these do not conflict but complement each other, also in the surroundings, we are in favor.

125696.explanation: We believe we should stop searching for a permanent asylum seeker center. With the emergency shelter, which so far seems to be running well, we are already more than meeting our responsibility.

125697.explanation: Employers also have a responsibility regarding the housing of their workers. Whether this is on their company site or not does not matter in this regard.

125713.explanation: Many more public order officers have been added in recent years.

125719.explanation: We make an exception for temporary residence.

125725.explanation: This is not solely the task of a municipality.

125727.explanation: The most important thing is that care is provided which demonstrably yields positive effects.

125737.explanation: Venray has already made considerable progress in this in recent years.

125739.explanation: Ultimately, the primary school decides on its own programming and curriculum. However, we do find the preservation of regional culture important, including our dialect.

125746.explanation: The center of Venray is already car-free.

125747.explanation: There must continue to be sheltered workplaces in Venray for people who cannot work in a regular workplace.

125689.explanation: Suitability and quality are leading, not gender or quotas.

125690.explanation: We choose participation and dialogue, not yes/no answers to complex issues.

125693.explanation: We offer customization, but no automatic permission without spatial consideration.

125694.explanation: We facilitate sustainable initiatives, but do not provide general subsidies.

125696.explanation: We honor the agreements with the COA, which means that the crisis emergency shelter may remain temporarily open. In this way, as Venray, we are doing more than enough. Unlike most other parties, however, we do not want a permanent asylum seekers’ center (AZC) in Venray. We want to stop that mandate from the municipal council as soon as possible.

125697.explanation: Housing only according to the rules and in appropriate locations, with good management and supervision of compliance with agreements.

125701.explanation: No executive role for the municipality; initiatives can be supported.

125705.explanation: No random building; housing construction must be planned and fit the landscape.

125706.explanation: Sports and development, especially for children, are a valuable investment.

125713.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and livability.

125714.explanation: Camera surveillance can be an effective and additional safety instrument. We do keep an eye on the privacy of our residents.

125715.explanation: We choose regional facilities and existing structures.

125719.explanation: This is not a structural solution to the housing shortage.

125721.explanation: Participation where possible; customization and transition to work are paramount.

125725.explanation: VVD Venray sees that charging points for electric cars are currently underused. Moreover, VVD Venray does not see providing charging points (just like gas stations, for example) as a municipal task.

125727.explanation: Good care is paramount; no separate care structures.

125731.explanation: Recently increased; do not increase further.

125732.explanation: But also attention to infill development. Expansion is for companies that provide social added value and quality employment. No more logistic boxes.

125733.explanation: But that does not automatically mean more will come; we just want fewer rules: entrepreneurs decide for themselves whether to open and are encouraged to coordinate well together.

125734.explanation: No extra priority beyond statutory duties.

125736.explanation: We follow the solar ladder. First roofs and paved surfaces; protection of the landscape. Where appropriate, VVD Venray wants to allow solar panels. However, the surroundings must be taken into account, so no solar fields allowed close to buildings.

125737.explanation: This is unaffordable; targeted investment where needed. Moreover, we have made other agreements with many users.

125738.explanation: Where parents can pay for it themselves, no subsidies are needed.

125739.explanation: Educational content is not the task of local government. But within national frameworks for schools.

125745.explanation: Where there is need, space must be created.

125746.explanation: It already is. A compact, attractive, and low-traffic center works.

125747.explanation: The municipality should not do this itself. But organize it well. Work must be organised regionally and suitably.

125749.explanation: Certainly at locations where it is now messy and very unhygienic.

125750.explanation: Fortunately, the F-35 will not come to Venray. But aside from that, the 5% defense norm offers opportunities for investments on and around the barracks. These developments can contribute to safety, employment, and the regional economy.

125689.explanation: It is not necessary, but diversity is also desirable for D66 Venray in the executive board.

125690.explanation: In the form of a corrective referendum. On the condition that at least two-thirds of Venray's eligible voters cast a vote.

125693.explanation: According to D66 Venray, there must be room for small-scale secondary activities that are subordinate to the main activity of a farm.

125694.explanation: Promoting the energy transition is important for D66 Venray. Financial support is intended for groups of residents or non-profit organizations and for activities such as energy saving and generating sustainable energy.

125696.explanation: The emergency shelter has been well organized for a long time in consultation with the neighborhood.

125697.explanation: D66 Venray stands for good housing for our international workers. It is not realistic that every entrepreneur, large or small, provides housing on their own site for all employees.

125701.explanation: From the turn of the year 2026 to 2027, a national ban on consumer fireworks will apply. As an alternative, the municipality can organize a fireworks show at a central location where people can meet and wish each other a happy new year.

125705.explanation: Through the Venray Space-for-Space scheme, farmers can exchange barns for houses. In addition, D66 Venray is happy to cooperate with initiatives to expand our cores with new homes.

125706.explanation: D66 Venray aims for the healthiest generation ever. People with fewer financial means should also be able to participate in sports at a sports club.

125713.explanation: More BOAs (special constables) are needed both in villages and rural areas. According to D66 Venray, they should receive more police tasks. Additionally, so-called green BOAs should be introduced to supervise our extensive natural areas.

125714.explanation: Provided that these are mobile so they can, for example, be deployed at events.

125715.explanation: D66 Venray wants more attention for safety at home. Prevention, such as education about early detection of domestic violence, is important. If that is not enough, a shelter for domestic violence victims should temporarily provide safety, also for people from other municipalities.

125719.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes seems like a short-term solution for the housing shortage but causes problems in the long term. Other solutions are more effective, and it is important not to undermine recreational stays.

125721.explanation: D66 Venray believes that people on welfare should primarily be helped to return to work as quickly as possible. For those unwilling to work, customized solutions should be applied.

125725.explanation: Yes, in the right places where people park their cars for longer periods or in neighborhoods where people cannot charge their car at home.

125727.explanation: D66 Venray finds it important that people, regardless of their faith, culture, or worldview, should be able to provide care to everyone who needs it.

125731.explanation: Tourist tax has recently been increased in the municipality of Venray.

125732.explanation: Adjacent to existing industrial sites such as Smakterheide in Venray, in Wanssum, and the Agrobaan in Ysselsteyn.

125733.explanation: Sunday shopping is allowed all year round. Retailers choose themselves on which Sundays they are open.

125734.explanation: Municipalities have the task to provide housing to people with residence permits. The distribution of status holders is determined by the national government every six months and depends on the number of people living in the municipality.

125736.explanation: The creation of some large-scale energy landscapes in the region can contribute to the energy transition. If farmers or landowners want to cooperate, D66 Venray will allow that.

125737.explanation: D66 Venray believes all villages and neighborhoods deserve good places for residents to meet each other. Almost all community centers in the villages have been renewed. Ysselsteyn, Geijsteren, and Castenray should follow, according to D66 Venray. New accommodation policy will be drawn up for the Venray core.

125738.explanation: This could be reimbursed to people with low income via Stichting Leergeld.

125739.explanation: D66 Venray has a warm heart for the Venrods dialect. But the municipality does not control education content. Schools decide for themselves whether or not to pay attention to it.

125745.explanation: In new neighborhoods, more space for parking places reduces space for new homes and public green areas. According to D66 Venray, cycling should be encouraged more by upgrading bike connections.

125746.explanation: The center of Venray is already car-free.

125747.explanation: D66 Venray believes people with work disabilities deserve a safe place to work.

125749.explanation: D66 Venray wants to reconsider the use of PMD bags, address bottlenecks, and switch where necessary. The new waste system has just been introduced; switching again soon to a new system would cause a significant increase in waste charges.

125750.explanation: No fighter jets will come to the former De Peel airbase. However, the military will expand here with air defense systems and personnel. This offers opportunities for the municipality of Venray.

125689.explanation: First and foremost, candidates must be suitable. Equal distribution would be nice, but the male/female ratio is of secondary importance.

125690.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make decisions. If a referendum is decided upon, it is crucial that the question is correctly defined and allows only one interpretation.

125693.explanation: Always allowing is too broadly formulated. It must fit the environment and not hinder the business operations of existing other companies. However, a municipality should support initiators with a 'yes unless' attitude instead of 'no unless'.

125694.explanation: This can lead to an unequal playing field: residents who do not participate in such a project—for example, because they rent, have little money, or have no suitable location—do not benefit from the subsidy, while they do contribute via taxes. If a project is only viable with subsidies, it is questionable whether it is sustainable and fair or efficient to support such a project.

125696.explanation: Venray must fulfill its share of the task it has to undertake.

125697.explanation: Preferably a separation between living and employer.

125701.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show is not the task of municipal government using taxpayers' money. It is also a factor that the show is not easily accessible for residents of the villages. A citizen initiative for a large fireworks show should be assessed willingly.

125705.explanation: Within existing rules, a farmer (or landowner) may build. It is important that there is a good quality of life for (future) residents. Random building on their land (which the statement seems to suggest) is not a good idea.

125706.explanation: Membership of a sports club can be paid from the participation budget that residents on welfare in the municipality of Venray receive. It is up to the resident to decide what that budget is used for. It may be for sports but also, for example, a public transport card.

125713.explanation: BOAs that only focus on safety are not needed. More BOAs who can focus on enforcement are indeed necessary.

125714.explanation: That does not have to be permanent but may be. Think of entertainment areas or places with a lot of nuisance. It may also be used to tackle illegal dumping of waste near containers.

125715.explanation: It is essential that there are sufficient shelters for domestic violence victims. This can be addressed regionally.

125719.explanation: This can contribute to solving the housing shortage. However, holiday homes must be suitable (also in terms of surroundings) for permanent residence.

125721.explanation: That also fits within the current policy of the municipality of Venray.

125725.explanation: There must be sufficient charging points in public spaces. But that fits within existing policy. No extra focus is needed. Often charging points remain empty while regular parking spots are full.

125727.explanation: Municipalities do not have full control over the supply of care providers. Care supply is already tight; additional requirements based on faith or culture could lead to waiting times or no available offer at all. A municipality cannot enforce that there is a suitable provider for every conviction.

125731.explanation: Match the average price used by regional municipalities.

125732.explanation: Companies currently established in Venray and that add value to our economy and employment should be able to expand within limits. More industrial sites are needed for that. Studies also show that need.

125733.explanation: Sunday shopping in Venray is already free and determined by entrepreneurs.

125736.explanation: Solar panels should not be placed on fertile agricultural land. That fertile land is needed for food production.

125737.explanation: That has largely already been done through the 'Clean through the Gate' program. Custom solutions are provided for community centers that have not yet participated.

125738.explanation: Since 1985, school swimming lessons are no longer mandatory. Learning to swim is the responsibility of the parents. Residents with low incomes can have swimming lessons fully or partially paid through the Leergeld foundation.

125739.explanation: Dialect is part of our identity. However, it is up to schools to decide whether to include it in their curricula. When there is a request for support from education, we can assess how a municipality can facilitate this.

125743.explanation: If what is meant is a local village facility, then we are in favor.

125745.explanation: This requires a tailored approach. If there really is a parking problem, a solution must be sought. Simply creating more parking spaces is pointless.

125746.explanation: The core shopping area of Venray is already car-free (except for permit holders such as residents). The rest of the center should remain accessible to cars.

125747.explanation: This is being worked on in Venray. The municipality should not become the owner of the company.

125749.explanation: The yellow bag causes nuisance and annoyance. A bin could be a solution.

125750.explanation: This is also included in the current Defense plans regarding De Peel. As far as we are concerned, this no longer involves flying the F35. That does not belong at De Peel.

125689.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

125690.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue is very important, a referendum or preferably a citizens' council can be a good and fair way to make a decision. This way, people are involved in local democracy.

125693.explanation: Agriculture must become significantly more sustainable, but we also want farmers to be able to earn money from their businesses. A shop with products from their own land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to make these options possible. Of course, this must fit within the environmental law.

125694.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity in the same place, so the grid is less burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

125696.explanation: We in Venray must also contribute our share to refugee reception. This must be fairly distributed among all municipalities, as regulated by the Distribution Act.

125697.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure they have decent housing. This can be on their own farm if it is near facilities, but can also be elsewhere nearby. Linking work and housing is not ideal because it creates dependency.

125701.explanation: A New Year's celebration is something you all celebrate together. The municipality will organize a light or laser show as an animal-friendly, healthy, and safe alternative to fireworks. Initially in Venray, but this can also be done at other locations in the municipality.

125705.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage, and an extra home on the property can be a good solution. As an additional source of income or as a suitable place for an elderly person who can no longer live independently. However, we do not want urbanization of the countryside, so the number must fit within the environmental plan.

125706.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and keep you healthy but are often too expensive for people on welfare. We want this group to be able to join a sports club for free. In Venray, this is already the case for people with incomes up to 120% of the minimum wage. We want to extend this scheme to a larger group.

125713.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOAs) on the streets make more people feel safe. They can intervene immediately if something happens.

125714.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we believe more cameras are not the right solution. Your privacy is a great good that should not be easily violated. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if needed.

125715.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A shelter for domestic violence victims is indispensable for our municipality.

125719.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

125721.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty. This causes new problems.

125725.explanation: Especially at apartment complexes, there are currently few charging points for electric cars. These are convenient places to designate additional charging points.

125727.explanation: Everyone should be able to receive care that suits them. For the care the municipality is responsible for, we provide as inclusive an offer as possible.

125731.explanation: The tourist tax in our municipality was already increased by nearly 50% in 2025. Increasing it further risks fewer tourists coming to Venray, leading to less income for our entrepreneurs. Fewer tourists are also not good for the livability of our center.

125732.explanation: There are enough business parks in the municipality, and Smakterheide is currently being further expanded. What can help is clustering agricultural companies. That prevents a messy countryside.

125733.explanation: We find it important that shops can open on Sundays for customers and shopkeepers who want that. This also makes it much easier for singles who work during the week to do important shopping.

125734.explanation: Status holders already have priority. We want to keep it this way because without priority for these people with residence permits, more asylum seeker centers would be needed and they would integrate less quickly into the community. We want to comply with the legal obligation.

125736.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want to, they should be able to use their land for this, as long as it fits within the area. This can be well combined with more biodiversity in the field through flowers and greenery and with keeping animals.

125737.explanation: Every village or neighborhood must have a good community center. Many community centers have already been renewed. Work is still being done on others or still needs to be done. Every village and neighborhood must have a good facility.

125738.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Also children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not arranged fairly. Every child should be able to swim by the time they finish primary school. We want children to have at least the A diploma.

125739.explanation: Schools may decide themselves how they want to pay attention to Limburgish. The Province of Limburg is working to ensure more possibilities arise, and the municipality wants this to be possible for all schools in our municipality.

125743.explanation: Every village already has a community center. We want to invest in that. For other catering and shops, it is up to the villages themselves to see which facilities are possible and whether there are entrepreneurs who want to run them.

125745.explanation: Venray already applies a generous parking standard. More parking spaces in the neighborhoods are not necessary.

125746.explanation: The center of Venray is already car-free. That should remain so. We want the speed within the ring roads everywhere adapted to cyclists and pedestrians. The maximum speed there is 30 km/h.

125747.explanation: NLW employees need a new place. Preferably we see a new sheltered work company started by the municipality of Venray. It may also be a branch in Venray of a sheltered work company from one of our neighboring municipalities. The most important thing is that these people quickly get a new sheltered workplace.

125749.explanation: Bags for plastic waste cause a lot of nuisance, for example in the center and in Brukske. We want a solution for this. Trash bins are a good replacement for the bags. In the neighboring municipality Horst aan de Maas this works well. For high-rise buildings, an underground container is a better solution.

125750.explanation: An increase in ground-based activities and air defense is possible. We are less happy with more flight movements at the airbase. This is bad for the environment and the extra noise nuisance makes living less pleasant for our residents. Fortunately, no F35s will come to Vredepeel.

125689.explanation: For the SP, equality is of great importance. This also includes at least proportional representation of women in our local government.

125690.explanation: The SP attaches great value to citizen participation; we also mean the possibility to talk along about important issues through a referendum.

125693.explanation: The SP is not opposed to farmers opening shops and campsites, but such plans should be reviewed individually.

125694.explanation: The SP supports a provincial energy company. This company can support sustainable initiatives.

125696.explanation: The SP advocates for humane and small-scale asylum accommodation. We support initiatives and measures to increase connection and support among and with residents.

125697.explanation: The SP believes employers are responsible for small-scale housing of labor migrants. It is important that the municipality strictly supervises compliance with rules and ensures good living conditions and a healthy living environment.

125705.explanation: The SP opposes allowing farmers to build homes on their own land because this leads to unequal opportunities, extra pressure on facilities, and the risk that agricultural land will disappear for housing and/or that speculation with agricultural land will occur.

125706.explanation: The SP believes in equal opportunities for everyone. People receiving social assistance benefits also have the right to participate in sports.

125713.explanation: The SP is in favor of more enforcement.

125714.explanation: The SP does not support the permanent use of camera surveillance due to privacy concerns. However, we are open to using temporary cameras in strategic locations.

125715.explanation: The SP finds it important that victims of domestic and gender-related violence can find a safe place in Venray.

125719.explanation: The SP disagrees with this because holiday homes are not designed for permanent residence and do not meet the required standards. Permanent residence can also put pressure on the tourist character of holiday homes.

125721.explanation: The SP thinks it is good if people are socially involved. However, this should not be a requirement for receiving social assistance benefits.

125725.explanation: The SP believes the current provision is more than sufficient. However, we do think there should be some charging points near apartment buildings.

125727.explanation: The SP is fine with people having preferences, but it is not always possible to meet them.

125731.explanation: The SP agrees with this. Moreover, we think it is important that the income is used for local infrastructure and tourism.

125732.explanation: The SP disagrees with this because there are already enough business parks in Venray.

125733.explanation: The SP disagrees with this because it does not benefit small entrepreneurs. Furthermore, entrepreneurs are free to open on Sundays.

125734.explanation: The SP finds it important that there is flow-through from shelters.

125736.explanation: The SP agrees with this because we want to make good use of roofs first before sacrificing important agricultural land.

125737.explanation: The SP supports the current plans to improve community centers.

125738.explanation: The SP is in favor of free school swimming.

125739.explanation: The SP considers Limburg culture important, but this responsibility lies with the province, not the municipality.

125745.explanation: The SP disagrees because it is not feasible to expand the number of parking spaces in existing neighborhoods. However, we do believe more parking spaces should be built at new homes.

125746.explanation: The SP thinks the center should be car-reduced, as it is now. Local entrepreneurs and people who live in the center should be able to use a car.

125747.explanation: The SP finds it important that sheltered work is possible and remains possible in Venray.

125749.explanation: The SP agrees with this because the current bags break quickly and cause a lot of litter and pests.

125750.explanation: The SP is not against the use of the airbase by Defence, as long as this does not cause (noise) nuisance.

125689.explanation: Diversity is important and can contribute to broader perspectives. However, this should never weigh heavier than suitability and quality. Leaders must be selected based on content and competence, not to fulfill symbolic politics around inclusivity and diversity.

125690.explanation: It is important that people are involved in major decisions. It must be clear what happens to their input and how it is taken into account in decision-making.

125693.explanation: Farmers should have space to diversify their businesses within reasonable rules. The municipality should not unnecessarily restrict that space. An exception applies to campsites: they should only be permitted in a regulated way, with a maximum of one or two per church village to prevent uncontrolled proliferation.

125694.explanation: Local energy initiatives that work on affordable or free energy deserve active support from the municipality. The municipality should not only facilitate them but also protect them against obstruction from established economic interests in the energy sector.

125696.explanation: The temporary emergency shelter must close, but the municipality has a legal obligation to provide shelter. Financial incentives currently determine policy and undermine quality. Only structural, humane shelter is safe and responsible, preferably in existing distribution halls and not on new construction land.

125697.explanation: Employers who employ labor migrants carry their own responsibility for appropriate, safe, and humane housing, which must not end immediately in case of illness or dismissal.

125701.explanation: A municipal fireworks show can become a nice new annual tradition as a safe and collective alternative to private fireworks, with less nuisance, less damage, and more togetherness.

125705.explanation: It is difficult to explain that land is someone's property but building on it would not be possible. Farmers must reasonably be allowed to build on their own land, within clear rules.

125706.explanation: Sports are important for health and must be accessible to everyone. Existing institutions and arrangements, including Stichting Leergeld and the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture, must be well utilized and, where necessary, supplemented with arrangements for those over 18 who currently fall outside coverage.

125713.explanation: More enforcers do not automatically lead to more safety. It is about better, visible, and structural deployment. Much of the current enforcement needs to be replaced. Too much enforcement can actually create distrust and reduce livability.

125714.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used where it is really necessary, such as locations with structural problems like illegal dumping. It must not become a general tool but a targeted measure.

125715.explanation: A shelter for victims of domestic violence must be accessible to everyone who is a victim of domestic violence, regardless of gender. Protection and safety must be central.

125719.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes can help reduce housing shortages, as long as there are clear conditions to properly regulate safety, livability, and appropriate use.

125721.explanation: No abuse of vulnerable people as free labor under coercion. When people do social work, they deserve fair compensation. Support should activate and offer perspective, not obligate or punish.

125725.explanation: Extra charging points on the street are not future-proof. Charging via cables is often a temporary solution and can lead to wasted investments if new technologies make it unnecessary. Priority should be given first to truly future-oriented solutions.

125727.explanation: People who need care should be able to choose a care provider that aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. Appropriate care increases trust, quality, and effectiveness, as long as it complies with the law and professional care standards.

125731.explanation: The municipality must generate income, and a small additional contribution per tourist is only a limited individual burden but collectively yields a substantial annual result. A few extra euros are still justifiable, but excessively high rates can have a negative impact on tourism and the economy.

125732.explanation: More business parks are not a given. Venray must first better utilize existing space and address vacancy to protect the landscape and livability. Expansion should only be considered responsibly when there is demonstrable need.

125733.explanation: More shopping Sundays are possible, but only if done in consultation and coordination with local entrepreneurs. Participation should be voluntary, with room for customization per sector and area, not mandatory.

125734.explanation: Priority based on status is not fair to people who have been waiting for a house for years. Municipalities receive extra funds when they house status holders. That must never determine who gets a home. Equal rules, registration, and acting from shelter and urgency only in case of real need are fairer.

125736.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land should only be allowed in exceptional cases, exclusively when farmers generate energy directly for their own business. This prevents agricultural land from disappearing due to large-scale solar parks.

125737.explanation: Where community centers are outdated, Venray should invest. Not all at once, but step by step and together with the people.

125738.explanation: School swimming increases children's safety and ensures every child can learn to swim, regardless of their parents' income. The municipality should support this to ensure no child is excluded.

125739.explanation: Attention to Limburgish is valuable but should not be mandatory. Schools should be able to choose what suits their students and educational vision.

125743.explanation: Venray already has a village center in most villages. The focus should be on good maintenance and use, not on the mandatory creation of new centers where there is no need. Customization per village is important.

125745.explanation: There is not a shortage everywhere. Some neighborhoods need extra parking spaces; others do not. Venray must look per neighborhood at what is needed and ensure tailored solutions, with attention to livability and safety.

125746.explanation: A completely car-free center is not practical for everyone. The center must remain accessible to residents, elderly, entrepreneurs, and emergency services. Improve safety and livability, but keep the center accessible.

125747.explanation: People with disabilities have been left uncertain due to the phase-out of NLW and false expectations. You don't solve that by outsourcing. The municipality must take responsibility itself for secure, suitable work and good guidance, not pass it on to the market.

125749.explanation: PMD bags cause litter and are inconvenient. Waste should be able to be disposed of when it arises, not only when the calendar says. Returning to underground containers is cleaner, more flexible, and more practical for all residents.

125750.explanation: The municipality does not decide on the use of De Peel airbase. It is positive that some F-35s are currently being relocated elsewhere. It is important that the Ministry of Defense informs residents timely and clearly about exercises and restores any damage quickly and carefully.

125689.explanation: The pursuit of diversity is good, but for BBB quality comes first. Leaders should be selected based on knowledge, experience, and suitability, not on gender. Equal opportunities are more important than imposed ratios.

125690.explanation: A referendum is a valuable instrument to directly involve residents in important decisions. According to BBB, a referendum does not need to be binding but must be taken seriously. However, it should not be held too often, as that would diminish its value.

125693.explanation: BBB wants to stimulate multifunctional agriculture. Side activities such as a shop or campsite can contribute to farmers’ earning capacity. The condition is that these activities do not pose obstacles for existing businesses in the area.

125694.explanation: BBB supports sustainable energy generation by residents. For joint initiatives, the municipality can assist, with financial support primarily provided through the national government or the province. BBB opposes wind turbines and solar panels on agricultural land; no support will be given for these.

125696.explanation: The pressure on our community is already disproportionately high with many labor migrants, accommodated Ukrainians, extensive mental health care facilities, and a TBS clinic. Venray has already done its fair share!

125697.explanation: According to BBB, entrepreneurs are responsible for good housing of the labor migrants they employ. Good housing with proper management is a plus. This does not necessarily mean it has to be on their own premises.

125701.explanation: BBB is against a nationwide fireworks ban. Since such a ban is very likely next year, we believe the municipality should organize a fireworks show.

125705.explanation: BBB supports various new forms of housing; for us, it is important that new homes in rural areas do not impose restrictions on existing businesses nearby.

125706.explanation: According to BBB, sports must be affordable for everyone. There are better solutions to address poverty.

125713.explanation: BBB values safety. Additional community enforcement officers can contribute to visibility, enforcement, and the feeling of safety in neighborhoods and villages, provided they are deployed practically and with a human approach.

125714.explanation: Camera surveillance can be useful in specific locations but is not a cure-all. BBB believes camera surveillance should only be used purposefully, with respect for privacy.

125715.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be able to receive help quickly and safely. BBB finds it important that this shelter is accessible and available nearby.

125719.explanation: BBB sees holiday homes as a possible solution to the housing shortage. Permanent residence should therefore be allowed, but only with temporary permits and clear rules, so that recreation and livability remain balanced.

125721.explanation: Participation in society is important. Socially meaningful work can contribute to structure, self-esteem, and participation, as long as everyone's abilities are taken into account.

125725.explanation: BBB believes the growth of electric driving should be monitored but must not come at the expense of parking space. Custom solutions per neighborhood are necessary.

125727.explanation: People should appreciate the high-quality care offered in the Netherlands; care does not need to adapt to everyone's wishes.

125731.explanation: Tourists use facilities and public spaces. BBB finds it logical that they contribute to this via tourist tax, so costs do not fall solely on residents.

125732.explanation: New business parks are not a given for BBB. Existing sites must first be optimally utilized. Only with demonstrable regional added value and need can expansion be considered.

125733.explanation: Especially small entrepreneurs do not always have the capacity to operate extra shopping Sundays. BBB wants to protect them from extra workload and unfair competition.

125734.explanation: The housing shortage is significant. BBB finds it indefensible that people who have waited for years are passed over. Housing of status holders must be arranged fairly and evenly, without priority.

125736.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production. BBB opposes covering agricultural land with solar panels and opts for roofs and other locations.

125737.explanation: Community centers are important but not every building needs a complete renovation. BBB chooses customization and targeted investment where necessary.

125738.explanation: Swimming skills are important for children's safety. BBB believes school swimming deserves support.

125739.explanation: Limburgish is part of our culture and identity. BBB finds it important that schools pay attention to it.

125745.explanation: Not every neighborhood needs extra parking space. BBB chooses tailor-made approaches: where parking pressure is truly a problem, the municipality should work with residents to find solutions.

125746.explanation: A completely car-free city center makes it less accessible for seniors, families, and entrepreneurs. BBB chooses accessibility and balance.

125747.explanation: BBB believes it is important that people with a work disability have a suitable workplace close to home where they can make a real and meaningful contribution to society. A municipal company can make this possible.

125749.explanation: The current system with PMD bags works well. BBB does not see replacement by bins as necessary. However, it is important to prevent litter and ensure bags are not hung up too early.

125750.explanation: Defense is important for our security. BBB believes Venray can contribute to this, provided there is attention to nuisance and the living environment. Defense developments can also create more employment opportunities.

statements.title: The municipality of Venray must accept that Defence will make increased use of De Peel airbase.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The residents of Venray must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Venray moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must start a new company where people with employment disabilities can work.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een nieuw bedrijf starten waar

titleParts.glossary: People who, due to illness or a disability, have difficulty obtaining paid work.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Venray must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
There must be more camera surveillance in Venray.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Venray must have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Venray must allow more business parks.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: All larger villages in the municipality should have their own village centre.
Venray must renovate all community centers.
The center of Venray must become car-free.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Limburgish language.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
Venray must replace the PMD waste bags (plastic) with waste bins for every household.
